rationale: My round-1 ask is ADDRESSED. The Background rewrite inverts the epistemics: the untracked working paper is no longer load-bearing evidence (verbatim quotes and section numbers dropped; both rationales restated self-contained and independently checkable against ms-panel-run's real sections), and naming it with an explicit 'untracked, not committed' disclaimer is honest provenance, not citation — dropping the name entirely would lose provenance for no gain. Re-verified grounding at bd898dfa against the actual tree: ADR-0037 §8 contains the exact 'plain reviewable files' phrase and the explicit no-signing anti-footgun boundary; the R3b extension framing is fair — it extends audit-not-prevention to transcription without claiming tamper-proofness, and the touchpoint keeps 'no signing … unchanged'. The .codex.log path is sound three ways: verdictFileRE (tally.go:33) requires .json$ so the log can never read as a malformed verdict or perturb round derivation; isCoLocatedReview classifies it non-source; co-located reviews are git-tracked with .log precedent (spec 092). §3's malformed-counts-as-missing backs the ladder's fail-closed step. All three deviations acceptable — B strictly improves gate integrity. Every other round-1 fidelity finding still holds.
findings: ROUND-1 ASK [R1] — ADDRESSED. The research-doc citation is decoupled (deviation C): quotes/§ numbers removed, the file explicitly labeled an untracked internal working document, and the two design rationales restated in the spec's own words. Naming an untracked file WITH the disclaimer is acceptable grounding because nothing in the spec now depends on the file's content — the interactive-operator/judged-substitution rationale is independently verifiable against ms-panel-run/SKILL.md's actual Launch/Codex-failure-detection sections, and the context-economy rationale is argued in-spec. Verified the file is still absent from the tree and all history at bd898dfa, exactly as the spec now discloses. The reference does not need to go entirely; the disclaimer is the fix.

NEW CLAIM VERIFIED — ADR-0037 §8 extension framing is fair. §8 ('Trust boundary: anti-footgun, not anti-adversary') literally says 'panel state is plain reviewable files' and forbids signing/hashing/out-of-repo state. R3b extends the audit-not-prevention principle to the transcription step (the .codex.log lets a human diff the recorded verdict against its source) without claiming adversary-proofness — the wrapper writes the log, so a motivated forger could doctor both, which is precisely the boundary §8 accepts (forgery leaves git fingerprints). The touchpoint explicitly keeps '§§6/8 … no signing … unchanged' and Out of Scope retains the §8 no-signing line. This also satisfies both halves of the round-1 R3-a alternatives: audit artifact AND honest trust-model placement.

NEW CLAIM VERIFIED — .codex.log path claims hold against the tree. (i) internal/panel/tally.go:33 verdictFileRE `^(.+)-round-([1-9][0-9]*)\.json$` cannot match `<slot>-round-<N>.codex.log`, so the audit log can never be tallied as a malformed verdict nor perturb §2 round derivation. (ii) internal/validate/divergence.go:344 isCoLocatedReview matches any `/reviews/` path, so the log classifies as a process artifact — no new ownership claim needed, consistent with the ADR-0037 2026-06-24 amendment's `<spec-dir>/reviews/**` matcher. (iii) 'Tracked' is real: co-located reviews dirs are git-tracked, including non-JSON .log files (spec 092 precedent). ADR-0037 §3's malformed-counts-as-missing rule independently backs the ladder's step-2 fail-closed semantics.

DEVIATION A (requirement numbered 3b) — ACCEPTABLE. Nothing machine-parses requirement numbers (internal/contextpack has no Requirements parsing; ParseSpec extracts Impacted Domains only), and keeping R4–R9 cross-references stable across panel rounds is a real reviewer benefit. R3b is referenced consistently in ADR Touchpoints, Scope, AC5, and the Manual proof.

DEVIATION B (ALLOWED_CLI stricter than asked) — ACCEPTABLE, an improvement. It converts a comment-defeatable negative grep into an allowlist declaration plus a literal-string ban; the four commands are exactly the CLI surface R1–R5 specify (file writes go through agent Write tools, so the allowlist does not under-provision); the residual 'positive greps satisfied by the declaration itself' is by design since the declaration IS the structural artifact and AC4 also requires the ALLOWED_CLI symbol. Side effect noted and fine: the workflow file can carry no prose fence about `mindspec complete` — the fence lives in R5/skills.
DEVIATION C (decouple, not commit) — ACCEPTABLE; it is my own ask's better resolution (see finding 1).

RE-VERIFIED (round-1 fidelity findings still hold at bd898dfa): workflow OWNERSHIP.yaml claims `.claude/skills/**` and `plugins/mindspec/**` but not `.claude/workflows/**` (R9 premise); installSkills exists (internal/setup/skills.go, called from claude.go:159) and embed.go carries //go:embed + SkillFiles() (R8 mirror source); TestWorkflowOwnsTraceAndGolangci exists (internal/validate/ownership_wave2_test.go:37); isDocFile/isProcessArtifact both return false for .claude/workflows/ms-panel.js (verified by reading both functions); ms-panel-run retains all five named sections and ms-panel-tally all four judgment sections including '## After a halt — recovery' and '## Escape hatch', so AC8's new greps pin real, present headings; ADR-0034/0035/0036/0027 all exist.

OBSERVATION (non-blocking, pre-existing, unchanged by the remediation): the ADR Touchpoints markdown links use `../../adr/…`, which from this spec's location (.mindspec/docs/specs/111-…) literally resolves to the nonexistent .mindspec/docs/adr/ in this mixed-layout tree (ADRs live flat at .mindspec/adr/). Merged spec 107 uses the identical convention, and the three-tier resolver spans both spec roots, so this is repo convention, not a 111 defect — but worth a glance at the post-109/110 rebase given 110 R6's ADR-touchpoint-must-resolve rule.

DISCLOSED LIMIT (unchanged from round 1): spec 109/110 claims (runner: key, panel verbs, verdict schema, claude_sub_on_quota) remain unverifiable on this base — both specs are absent from this tree — but the spec discloses them as hard prerequisites with a rebase-forward-before-plan-approve gate, mirroring the 108→107 pattern.
